mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and rising recession probability, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand resilience, supporting corporate revenue forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative median 60-day returns, or the interaction term loses predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) in a multivariate regression framework.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production indicates strengthening supply-side capacity and operational efficiency, which reduces input cost pressures and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during disinflationary periods.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0% year-over-year, or the mechanism shows negative alpha in high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level above 5.0% signals restrictive monetary policy that suppresses risk appetite and increases borrowing costs, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are non-negative in > 70% of observations above the threshold, or the signal exhibits negative predictive accuracy in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly gains in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, boosting consumer spending expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative median 60-day returns, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) when controlling for monetary policy shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding economic output and corporate revenue potential, which directly translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward returns, or the Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production coinciding with elevated inflation signals a negative supply shock that disrupts production chains and raises costs, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days during inflationary regimes.
failure_condition: SPX yields positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances during high-inflation periods, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in out-of-sample validation.
